metadata.format_notes: User messages are verbatim. Assistant responses are substantive summaries (actual responses were often long; full decisions captured in PROPOSAL.md and proposal-critique-v03-revision.md). Tool-use descriptions omitted; only conversational exchange captured.

turns.user: read and follow tmp/v004-critique-interview-prompt.md
turns.assistant: Read the prompt instructing a v004 critique-and-interview pass against v003 PROPOSAL.md, using the v002/v003 failure-mode framework (ambiguity/malformation/incompleteness/incorrectness) and the multi-proposal file format. Read all required files: PROPOSAL.md, livespec-nlspec-spec.md, goals-and-non-goals.md, subdomains-and-unsolved-routing.md, prior-art.md, bash-skill-script-style-requirements.md, the three lifecycle diagram files, terminology summary, and history from v001/v002/v003 (critique + revision). Produced 'Proposal Critique v03' structured per prior critique pattern: 8 Major Gaps (M1-M8), 6 Significant Gaps (S9-S14), 7 Cleanups (C15-C21), labeled by failure mode. Saved to proposed_changes/v004-proposed-change-proposal-critique-v03.md in multi-proposal format (file-level front-matter + flat `## Proposal:` sections with Target/Summary/Motivation/Proposed Changes).

turns.assistant_initiative: Created 21 tracking tasks (one per item). Began interview with M1 (schemas/ directory missing from skill layout), printing sub-options visibly before asking.
turns.user: [M1 answered: Accept as proposed — add schemas/ to skill-layout tree with critique-findings.json as representative file.]

turns.assistant: Marked M1 complete. Moved to M2 (livespec-nlspec-spec.md location contradiction — skill-root vs template-root vs prompts/ typo).
turns.user: [M2 answered: Accept — template-root only; remove skill-root entry; fix prompts/ typo.]

turns.assistant: Moved to M3 (sub-command dispatch script unnamed). Printed four sub-options: scripts/dispatch.sh, different name, inline in SKILL.md, or leave unspecified.
turns.user: Is this the single entry point for ALL scripts in the skill? If so, should it be called something like main?

turns.assistant: Clarified: no, it's not the single entry point for ALL scripts. Two distinct scripts exist — the dispatcher (entry point for user invocations) and doctor-static.sh (invoked from inside command files). `main` would mislead because doctor-static is called directly. Reformulated options: (a) two scripts (dispatch.sh + doctor-static.sh), (b) single mega-script main.sh, (c) dispatch inline in SKILL.md.
turns.user: [M3 answered: Accept — two scripts: dispatch.sh + doctor-static.sh.]

turns.assistant: Moved to M4 (bundled-default propose-change prompt location). Printed three options: REQUIRED, OPTIONAL-with-fallback path, or no-prompt simplification.
turns.user: What is the purpose of this prompt? How does the proposed change use it? How does that relate to the JSON interface contract?

turns.assistant: Explained: the prompt's job is raw intent text → JSON findings array. Used by propose-change when input is raw text; bypassed when input is already structured findings (from critique). Output contract is identical to critique.md's output — both emit the same findings schema. So prompt is a reshape-only step. Reframed options: (a) REQUIRED at template level (parallel with critique), (b) OPTIONAL with skill-level fallback, (c) drop prompt entirely; skill treats raw text as single proposal.
turns.user: [M4 answered: REQUIRED at template level.]

turns.assistant: Moved to M5 (critique → propose-change invocation mode: sub-command call vs internal function call). Options: (a) internal delegation, (b) sub-command re-invocation, (c) shared helper both call.
turns.user: [M5 answered: internal delegation.]

turns.assistant: Moved to M6 (shared/ directory '...'). Options: (a) collapse to prose, (b) enumerate fully, (c) remove shared/ entirely.
turns.user: [M6 answered: collapse to prose.]

turns.assistant: Moved to M7 (pre-step doctor auto-backfill blast radius). Options: (a) move auto-backfill to explicit `doctor --backfill`, (b) gate with --allow-backfill, (c) keep current auto-behavior.
turns.user: Wouldn't it be more straightforward to have the backfill only work against the committed state of the current specification?

turns.assistant: Agreed — cleaner semantics. Traced through: current design compares working tree vs history (skipping on WIP); proposed design compares `git show HEAD:` committed state vs history. Key improvement: fixes the hole where uncommitted WIP + previously-committed out-of-band drift would silently skip detection. Split M7 into two interacting decisions: (M7a) comparison target, (M7b) auto-backfill vs opt-in.
turns.user: [M7a answered: committed state via `git show HEAD:`.]

turns.assistant: Moved to M7b. With committed-state semantics in place, reformulated: (a) auto-backfill in pre-step retained, (b) doctor --backfill opt-in only, (c) fail with manual paths.
turns.user: I don't understand what you mean by this "cost is user-preferred topic names are lost when user combines direct-commit + propose-change."   How does the proposed change even relate to this? Want me to go through step by step and stop being so obtuse?

turns.assistant: Retracted the 'user-preferred topic names lost' claim. Traced through the actual scenario step-by-step: user edits spec.md, commits, runs `propose-change my-topic 'different intent'`; pre-step backfills the committed diff under out-of-band-edit-<UTC>; aborts; user commits, re-runs; propose-change then creates my-topic.md for the NEW intent. The committed diff and the new intent are two separate proposals — my 'cost' claim was inventing a conflict that doesn't exist. Re-posed the cleaner tradeoff: auto-backfill writes history/v(N+1)/ before the user's sub-command runs (philosophical surprise) vs opt-in requires explicit doctor --backfill (no mutation without consent).
turns.user: I like option one with the auto backfill, but it is still going to abort whatever command they were trying to run and tell them to commit the auto-generated files, correct?

turns.assistant: Confirmed: option 1 retains the abort-and-rerun flow. Pre-step detects drift → auto-backfill writes history/v(N+1)/ with spec snapshot and the captured proposed-change+revision pair moved inside → exits nonzero → outer sub-command aborts → user commits the history → re-runs. Recorded M7b as auto-backfill.
turns.user: [M7b answered: auto-backfill in pre-step (with committed-state semantics from M7a).]

turns.assistant: Moved to M8 (doctor exit codes conflict with bash style doc). Options: (a) update PROPOSAL.md to 0/1/3, (b) relax bash style doc, (c) doctor-specific custom code.
turns.user: [M8 answered: update PROPOSAL.md to 0/1/3.]

turns.assistant: Moved to S9 (plugin.json schema undefined). Options: (a) delegate to Claude Code's current format, (b) pin a version/URL, (c) author minimal schema in PROPOSAL.md.
turns.user: Re-read brainstorming/approach-2-nlspec-based/bash-skill-script-style-requirements.md, I just rewrote it with some updates

turns.assistant: Re-read the rewritten bash style doc. Summarized the significant additions: module structure (single-responsibility per library, prefix-based naming, forbidden catch-all filenames), purity/I/O isolation (pure vs impure classification, impure wrapper pattern, no global variable writes from functions), complexity thresholds (CCN ≤ 7, LLOC ≤ 50, file length ≤ 300, argument count ≤ 4), expanded testability (sourceable guard, bats-assert + bats-support, BATS_TEST_TMPDIR), code coverage with kcov (100% pure, 80% overall), expanded enforcement (pre-commit + CI architectural checks under scripts/ci/). Exit code contract unchanged — M8 still holds. Noted implications: M3 needs revision (executables MUST NOT carry .sh extension, so scripts/dispatch and scripts/doctor-static without extension), plus new gaps — G22 (scripts/bash-boilerplate.sh missing from layout), G23 (scripts/ci/ missing), G24 (doctor-static.sh violates extension rule; folds into M3), G25 (testing tooling bats-assert/kcov not surfaced in PROPOSAL.md), G26 (tests/fixtures/ + tests/ tree not shown). Asked how to handle the scope change.
turns.user: [Answer: Add G22-G26 to critique + revise M3.]

turns.assistant: Appended G22, G23, G25, G26 as new `## Proposal` sections in the critique file (G24 folded into M3 revision). Added 4 tasks. Returned to S9.
turns.user: [S9 answered: delegate to Claude Code's current format.]

turns.assistant: Moved to S10 (check #13 redundant with check #2). Options: (a) merge #13 into #2, (b) narrow each to distinct stages, (c) keep both.
turns.user: [S10 answered: merge into check #2.]

turns.assistant: Moved to S11 (prune-history `first` semantics on repeat prunes). Walked through concrete example: first prune produces marker at v003 with pruned_range [1,3]; second prune at N=9 deletes that marker. Is the new `first` = 1 (preserved original) or 3 (old marker's own version)? Options: (a) preserve original, (b) use marker version, (c) track both fields explicitly.
turns.user: [S11 answered: preserve original earliest.]

turns.assistant: Moved to S12 (testing implementation language not pinned). Options: (a) merge with G25 into one Testing rewrite, (b) keep as base S12 with G25 layered on, (c) leave language unpinned.
turns.user: [S12 answered: merge S12 + G25 into one update.]

turns.assistant: Moved to S13 (propose-change input-shape discrimination). Options: (a) explicit --findings-json flag (no sniffing), (b) try-parse JSON + schema-validate, (c) leave to implementer.
turns.user: [S13 answered: explicit --findings-json flag.]

turns.assistant: Moved to S14 (revise file and proposal ordering). Options: (a) lex order + document order, delegation per-file; (b) creation-time order for files; (c) delegation spans whole revise.
turns.user: c but still process files in creation order.

turns.assistant: Recorded S14: files by created_at (oldest first, lex fallback); proposals in document order; delegation spans all remaining proposals across files. Moved to C15 (duplicate 'no open questions' rule in propose-change section and format section). Options: (a) keep in format, delete from propose-change, (b) reverse, (c) keep both.
turns.user: [C15 answered: keep in format section, delete from propose-change.]

turns.assistant: Moved to C16 (seed idempotency edge cases). Options: (a) config separate from targets, partial state = refusal; (b) strict clean-slate including config; (c) add --force flag.
turns.user: [C16 answered: config separate; partial state = refusal.]

turns.assistant: Moved to C17 (tighten history/vNNN/README.md tree annotations). Options: (a) tighten, (b) reject.
turns.user: [C17 answered: tighten annotations.]

turns.assistant: Moved to C18 (project-root tests location clarification). Options: (a) tighten into merged Testing rewrite, (b) reject.
turns.user: [C18 answered: tighten into merged Testing rewrite.]

turns.assistant: Moved to C19 (revision-steering-intent content injection conflict with no-other-ingress-paths rule). Options: (a) forbid content injection in steering-intent, (b) loosen the ingress invariant, (c) keep soft discouragement.
turns.user: [C19 answered: forbid content injection.]

turns.assistant: Moved to C20 (section-drift meta-test naming inconsistency in DoD). Options: (a) use full name test_meta_section_drift_prevention.bats in DoD, (b) shorten everywhere, (c) leave as-is.
turns.user: [C20 answered: use full name test_meta_section_drift_prevention.bats in DoD.]

turns.assistant: Moved to C21 (pre-step skip warning channel). Options: (a) LLM narration via structured finding, (b) stderr only, (c) both.
turns.user: [C21 answered: LLM narration via structured finding.]

turns.assistant: Started the four items added mid-interview. Moved to G22 (scripts/bash-boilerplate.sh missing from skill layout). Options: (a) add to layout, (b) keep at bash-doc level only.
turns.user: What do you think would be the best practice based on bash best practices? The boilerplate is just something I came up with in the pre-AI days. It's not necessarily canonical, or I don't know if it's the best approach.

turns.assistant: Gave honest analysis. Shared boilerplate pattern is defensible but not canonical — bash world typically uses single-script templates (Bash3Boilerplate, etc.) copied per script, focused shared libraries for specific concerns, or framework-based tools (Bashly, Shellspec). Costs: source-path fragility (the $(dirname ${BASH_SOURCE[0]})/bash-boilerplate.sh dance), awkward bootstrap (script MUST set -e before sourcing), test-sourcing interactions, potential version drift. Benefits: DRY, automatic uniformity, suits small-bundle all-ships-together discipline. Recommended per-script copy-paste + CI verification as closer to canonical. Reformulated options: (a) per-script + CI, (b) keep shared boilerplate + add to layout, (c) hybrid focused libs.
turns.user:
Your recommendation adds CI complexity and adds duplication to every script.

Defend this claim. Is it really brittle and error-prone? Doesn't bash just work the way it works? Under what scenarios would this fail? Give me something concrete.:

'1. Source-path fragility — the $(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")/bash-boilerplate.sh or realpath dance is error-prone, especially across scripts/ci/ subdirectories.'

turns.assistant: Retracted the fragility claim. Walked through concrete scenarios: absolute invocation works, relative works, bash scripts/dispatch works, sourced from another script works, symlink handled by realpath. Actual failure modes: author moves script without updating ..-count (caught loudly on first run), missing boilerplate (loud failure on first source line), exotic invocations (bash -c with content on stdin). None are silent/subtle. The 'brittleness' is purely author-error, caught immediately. Admitted the user's original read was right: per-script copy-paste + CI verification adds real complexity (new CI check script) and real duplication to solve a problem that isn't there. Revised recommendation: keep the shared boilerplate. Reformulated options: (a) add to layout, (b) reject.
turns.user: [G22 answered: add to layout.]

turns.assistant: Moved to G23 (scripts/ci/ directory missing from layout). Options: (a) add to layout, (b) keep at bash-doc level only.
turns.user: Why are we using the directory name ci? This should be able to run locally via git hook too, right?

turns.assistant: Confirmed the user's point. The bash style doc lists the same architectural checks under both pre-commit hooks AND CI sections — they're invoked by both. 'ci/' misnames them as CI-only. Better names: scripts/checks/ (clean, accurate), scripts/gates/ (jargon-y), scripts/architectural-checks/ (explicit but long). Recommended scripts/checks/. Noted the rename also applies to the bash style doc (3 references there). Reformulated options: scripts/checks/, scripts/gates/, scripts/architectural-checks/, keep scripts/ci/.
turns.user: [G23 answered: scripts/checks/.]

turns.assistant: Moved to G25 (strengthen Testing section with bats-assert + kcov). Noted already slated to merge with S12/G26/C18 into one Testing rewrite. Options: (a) name all tooling in PROPOSAL.md, (b) bats-core only; rest deferred to style doc.
turns.user: [G25 answered: name all tooling in PROPOSAL.md.]

turns.assistant: Moved to G26 (tests/ directory structure). Options: (a) show skeleton tree, (b) flat tests/ without scripts/ subdir, (c) reject; rely on bash style doc.
turns.user: [G26 answered: show skeleton tree.]

turns.assistant: Interview complete. Summarized all 25 decisions (21 original + 4 mid-interview additions). Began dogfood lifecycle apply: wrote history/v004/proposed_changes/proposal-critique-v03-revision.md with per-item decisions and rationale; rewrote top-level PROPOSAL.md to v004 incorporating all decisions (schemas/ added to layout, livespec-nlspec-spec.md canonicalized at template root, scripts renamed without .sh, bash-boilerplate.sh + scripts/checks/ added, shared/ collapsed to prose, prompts/propose-change.md REQUIRED, critique internal delegation clarified, check #9 uses committed-state comparison, exit codes 0/1/3, plugin.json delegated to Claude Code's format, check #13 merged into #2 (now 12 checks), prune-history first semantics specified, propose-change --findings-json flag, revise creation-time + whole-revise delegation, <revision-steering-intent> forbids content injection, DoD test name fixed, pre-step warning channel specified, Testing section fully consolidated with bats-assert/bats-support/kcov/fixtures/BATS_TEST_TMPDIR + tests/ tree skeleton); copied PROPOSAL.md byte-identical to history/v004/PROPOSAL.md; moved critique to history/v004/proposed_changes/proposal-critique-v03.md; renamed scripts/ci/ → scripts/checks/ in bash style doc (2 occurrences); updated history/README.md with v004 entry; captured this conversation as history/v004/conversation.json.
